frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Recent stimuli bias current perception toward prior input.

frameworks.name: perceptual prior / temporal regularity exploitation
frameworks.one_line: Brain leverages short-timescale world stability to form expectations.

frameworks.name: sensory cortex representation (V1 orientation decoding)
frameworks.one_line: Behavioral bias linked to bias in early visual cortex activity patterns.

core_question: Does the perceptual bias known as serial dependence have a neural correlate in primary visual cortex orientation representations?
key_assumptions: Short-timescale stimulus regularities shape perceptual decisions
fMRI activity patterns in V1 carry decodable orientation information
Previous-trial reports can bias current-trial percepts
manipulations: Visual-field location of grating (left vs right)
key_findings: Reported orientation on the current trial was biased toward the previously reported orientation
V1 orientation signal was similarly biased toward the orientation presented on the previous trial
Both perceptual and neural biases were spatially specific to the same visual-field location
interpretations: Biases in perceptual decisions induced by recent stimuli may arise from neural biases in sensory cortex shaped by recent perceptual history
